rationale: Stremersch et al. (2003) is cited multiple times in the theory development, not just in a passing background list. The citing paper uses it to position the existing MCS literature, to anchor its modularity construct definition, and to support arguments about buyer mix-and-match choices and monitoring-related implications in modular systems. Even though some citations are grouped, the paper assigns this source a meaningful role in framing and hypothesis logic.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Stremersch et al. (2003) as a conceptual anchor for research on buying modular multicomponent systems, especially around modularity and mix-and-match purchasing choices. It then builds its own contract-specificity and monitoring-dispute framework on top of that prior foundation.
